title: triticum turgidum var. compositum
intro: Triticum turgidum var. compositum, also known as wheat or emmer, is an ancient grain that belongs to the wheat family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues. The health benefits of consuming Triticum turgidum var. compositum include improved gut health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced nutrient intake. In herbal preparations, it can be made into flour for baking or used as a tea to reap its medicinal benefits.
